summary: Today's sweep leans toward Democracy / press freedom, Education / pedagogy, AI / computing. The strongest items in this run are Middle East crisis live: Iran says it has seized two ships in strait of Hormuz after Trump extends ceasefire, How Bolivia’s cacao farmers took on the gold-mining industry – and won, and Adaptable AI Lessons with CSTA Responsible AI Fellow Tim Swick.

mainItems.title: Middle East crisis live: Iran says it has seized two ships in strait of Hormuz after Trump extends ceasefire
mainItems.summary: State media says the ships have been transferred to Iran’s coast Trump announces ceasefire extension Why Trump’s erratic commentary is the real block to an Iran deal If you’re just joining us, here’s the main news of the day. It is 9.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: How Bolivia’s cacao farmers took on the gold-mining industry – and won
mainItems.summary: As rising gold prices fuel environmental destruction, communities in the country’s biodiverse heartland are passing laws against mining Mahogany trees tower above Herminio Mamani as he tends his cacao farm in Bolivia ’s biodiverse…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: Adaptable AI Lessons with CSTA Responsible AI Fellow Tim Swick
mainItems.summary: Tim Swick is a teacher for Lexington Richland School District 5 in South Carolina, where he is currently developing an...
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, classroom practice, and educational judgment.

mainItems.title: Nebraska Passes Special Ed Bill Without Proposed Protections for Students
mainItems.summary: This story was co-published with The Flatwater Free Press, Nebraska’s first independent, nonprofit newsroom focused on investigations and feature stories that matter. For Dave Murman, the issue was personal.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, classroom practice, and educational judgment.

mainItems.title: Anthropic’s most dangerous AI model just fell into the wrong hands
mainItems.summary: Anthropic's Mythos AI model, a powerful cybersecurity tool that the company said could be dangerous in the wrong hands, has been accessed by a "small group of unauthorized users," Bloomberg reports.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to the shape of current AI systems and where the tooling is moving.

mainItems.title: Internal emails show how Amazon raises prices across the Internet, lawsuit says
mainItems.summary: “It’s working!“: Amazon emails with vendors show pattern of disappearing deals.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Touches trust, surveillance, and the private use of AI systems.

mainItems.title: The next Frostpunk game isn't a city builder, 11 Bit Studios reveal, alongside a modern "reimagining" of survival game This War Of Mine
mainItems.summary: 11 Bit Studios have confirmed that they're making a brand new Frostpunk , alongside a "full" reimagining of their 2014 survival story This War Of Mine , and two other "completely new" games codenamed P14 and P12.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth your time if you care about design, criticism, and not just hype.

mainItems.title: This Is a Gardening Show review – Zach Galifianakis’ charming new series feels like perfect TV
mainItems.summary: Part lesson, part lark, these 15-minute episodes are a total joy.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth watching for authoritarian drift, selective enforcement, and democratic norms.

mainItems.title: Framework's CEO on the RAM crisis and creating a "MacBook Pro for Linux users"
mainItems.summary: "We actually have slightly more Linux users than Windows users."
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to humane computing, open-source practice, and repairability.

mainItems.title: Opinion: School Vouchers Fail the Civil Rights Test. The Federal Program Is No Exception
mainItems.summary: Our public education system rests on a foundation of civil rights protections. Public schools exist to serve every child.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, student agency, and school policy.

mainItems.title: Startups Brag They Spend More Money on AI Than Human Employees
mainItems.summary: A new class of AI startups say they are taking money that would normally be used to hire people and are spending it on AI compute instead.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

mainItems.title: 'We can do better,' FAA head says of work to update U.S. analog air traffic system
mainItems.summary: The U.S. aviation system is being modernized — but FAA Administrator Bryan Bedford and other leaders say it will take more money to make the system more efficient and flexible.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Interesting where engineering, science, and tech infrastructure collide.

mainItems.title: Trump's order is a milestone for proponents of using psychedelics as medicine
mainItems.summary: President Trump has signed an executive order aimed at accelerating research and approval of psychedelics for treating mental health conditions, like depression, PTSD and addiction.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

highlights.title: A.I. Is Prompting an Evolution, Not Extinction, for Coders
highlights.notes: Interesting piece on the current state of AI in software work.

highlights.title: The Workers Letting A.I. Do Their Jobs
highlights.notes: In an era of agents powered by artificial intelligence, many programmers are barely programming.

highlights.title: Trump’s presidency is what evil looks like: absurd, frightening, cruel
highlights.notes: Particularly strong Guardian opinion piece, noted for future briefing highlights.
